data.description: In a situation where the outcome of an ability check is uncertain, a character's proficiency in a specific aspect is represented by their ability score and skill modifier. When making an ability check to overcome a challenge, the character or monster's proficiency in that particular skill is reflected in a modified roll against a difficulty class (DC). The outcome of such checks can be influenced by various bonuses and penalties applied before rolling, as well as through the application of features or abilities that allow for doubling or multiplying the proficiency bonus. In contests between characters or monsters, each participant applies their appropriate ability score modifier, bonuses, and penalties to their check, and the one with the higher total wins. Ties result in a continuation of the contest until a winner is determined.
